title: A Letter to Her Husband by Anne Bradstreet — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
Absent upon Public Employment

My head, my heart, mine eyes, my life, nay more,
My joy, my magazine, of earthly store,
If two be one, as surely thou and I,
How stayest thou there, whilst I at Ipswich lie?
So many steps, head from the heart to sever,
If but a neck, soon should we be together.
meaning: "A Letter to Her Husband" by Anne Bradstreet is a sad and love and nature poem consisting of 28 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and love and nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Absent upon Public Employment ...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Anne Bradstreet's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
